description: Future 'so' locates the clause's event or state after the present moment, while intention and certainty remain separate. Present time is unmarked; 'to' (PST) locates an event before now.
articulatory_notes: Air passes without voicing through a narrow tongue groove for 's'. The tongue then draws back as the lips round for true-mid 'o'; the single syllable carries full stress.
usage_notes: Tense is first in Slot 1. Combinations such as 'so ki' (will have done) and 'so pa' (will begin) keep 'so' before the aspect particle. A clause has only one tense marker.
